title: Serpocaulon attenuatum
taxonomy: Serpocaulon attenuatum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The order Polypodiales is the next level of classification, further grouping the plant with other related ferns. Within the order, Serpocaulon attenuatum is placed in the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships.

intro: Serpocaulon attenuatum is a type of fern, specifically a species of the Polypodiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
